title: Arithmetic
description: Learn and master the concepts of Arithmetic with structured lessons, interactive visualizations, and practice exercises.

title: Pre-Algebra
description: This is where students transition from arithmetic to algebraic thinking.

title: Algebra I
description: Learn and master the concepts of Algebra I with structured lessons, interactive visualizations, and practice exercises.

title: Algebra II
description: This builds on Algebra I and prepares students for Precalculus and Calculus.

title: Geometry
description: Learn and master the concepts of Geometry with structured lessons, interactive visualizations, and practice exercises.

title: Trigonometry
description: Learn and master the concepts of Trigonometry with structured lessons, interactive visualizations, and practice exercises.

title: Precalculus
description: This course bridges **Algebra II + Trigonometry → Calculus I**.

title: Calculus I
description: This is the first true calculus course.

title: Calculus II
description: Calculus II focuses on **advanced integration, sequences & series, and parametric/polar calculus**.

title: Calculus III (Multivariable Calculus)
description: This course extends calculus from **one variable** to **multiple variables**.

title: Linear Algebra
description: This is one of the most important mathematics courses for engineering, computer science, AI, graphics, robotics, physics, and data science.

title: Differential Equations
description: This course studies equations involving derivatives and models real-world systems such as population growth, circuits, mechanics, and control systems.

title: Discrete Mathematics
description: This is the foundation of **Computer Science**, **Algorithms**, **Cryptography**, and **Logic**.

title: Probability
description: This course is the mathematical foundation for **Statistics, Machine Learning, AI, Data Science, Finance, Risk Analysis, and Scientific Research**.

title: Statistics
description: This is the standard university-level Statistics course and follows naturally after Probability.

title: Number Theory
description: Number Theory is the study of **integers**. It is fundamental to **cryptography**, **computer science**, and **pure mathematics**.

title: Graph Theory
description: Graph Theory is one of the most important subjects in **Computer Science, AI, Networking, Optimization, and Mathematics**.

title: Real Analysis
description: This is the course where mathematics becomes **rigorous**. Instead of just calculating derivatives and integrals, students prove *why* calculus works.

title: Abstract Algebra
description: Abstract Algebra studies **algebraic structures** such as groups, rings, and fields. It is foundational for modern mathematics, cryptography, coding theory, and theoretical computer science.

title: Complex Analysis
description: This is the study of **functions of complex numbers**. It is one of the most elegant areas of mathematics and has applications in physics, electrical engineering, fluid dynamics, signal processing, and quantum mechanics.
